trips.name: Grossglockner High Alpine Road
trips.description: Drive Austria's most legendary alpine pass, ascending to 2,571m with sweeping views of the Grossglockner — Austria's highest peak. The route winds through the Hohe Tauern National Park past glaciers, ibex herds, and the Franz-Josefs-Höhe viewpoint overlooking the Pasterze Glacier.
trips.cover_search_query: Grossglockner High Alpine Road Austria mountain pass glacier

trips.name: Wachau Danube Valley
trips.description: Follow the Danube through the UNESCO-listed Wachau Valley, a landscape of vineyard terraces, medieval abbeys, and cliff-top castle ruins. Sample world-class Grüner Veltliner wines, visit the opulent Melk Abbey, and explore the romantic ruin of Dürnstein Castle where Richard the Lionheart was once imprisoned.
trips.cover_search_query: Wachau Valley Danube river Austria vineyards Dürnstein

trips.name: Salzkammergut Lake District
trips.description: Wind through Austria's fairy-tale lake district, a mosaic of turquoise alpine lakes flanked by dramatic limestone peaks. Hallstatt — perhaps the world's most photographed village — clings to a clifftop above its mirror-still lake, while Wolfgangsee and Grundlsee offer serene beauty and excellent hiking.
trips.cover_search_query: Hallstatt lake Austria alpine village reflection

trips.name: Innsbruck & the Tyrolean Alps
trips.description: Explore the heart of Tyrol from its imperial capital, where the Old Town's golden roof and baroque façades are framed by razor-edged alpine peaks. Drive south to the Europabrücke, west to the Seefeld plateau, and north through the Karwendel wilderness for some of Austria's most dramatic mountain scenery.
trips.cover_search_query: Innsbruck Austria Old Town alpine mountains Nordkette

trips.name: Ötztal Alpine Drive
trips.description: Follow the Ötztal valley deep into the heart of the Alps, past thundering waterfalls and ice-age glaciers to the village of Sölden — home to some of Europe's highest ski terrain. The valley rises through pine forests and high meadows to the Rettenbach and Tiefenbach glaciers at over 3,000 metres.
trips.cover_search_query: Ötztal valley Austria glacier Sölden alpine road

trips.name: Zillertal & Krimml Waterfalls
trips.description: Drive up the cheerful Zillertal, famous for its brass bands and cable cars, then cross the Gerlos Alpine Road to reach the Krimml Waterfalls — the highest in Austria and among the tallest in Europe, plunging 380 metres through a roaring gorge of mossy boulders.
trips.cover_search_query: Krimml Waterfalls Austria Hohe Tauern highest waterfall

trips.name: Carinthian Lake Circuit
trips.description: Carinthia's lake district basks in the warmest, sunniest climate in Austria — a Mediterranean mood among alpine peaks. Circle the Wörthersee, Faaker See, and Ossiacher See, all with crystal-clear swimming water, then visit Klagenfurt's Renaissance old town and the island-crowned Burg Hochosterwitz fortress.
trips.cover_search_query: Wörthersee Carinthia Austria lake alpine summer

trips.name: Arlberg & Vorarlberg Mountains
trips.description: Austria's westernmost corner is its most dramatic — the Arlberg massif divides the Rhine valley from the Alps, and the Flexen Road snakes through tunnels and snow galleries above Zürs and Lech. This loop takes in Bregenz on Lake Constance, the Bregenzerwald's rolling cheese country, and the legendary ski resort of St. Anton.
trips.cover_search_query: Arlberg Austria mountain road Lech Zürs alpine winter

trips.name: Dachstein & Schladming Highlands
trips.description: The Dachstein massif straddles Styria and Upper Austria, its ice caves and high plateaus contrasting with the lush Ennstal below. Drive the scenic Grimming Road, visit Ramsau's dramatic cliffside church, and ride the Dachstein cable car to the Sky Walk for 360° views of five countries.
trips.cover_search_query: Dachstein glacier Austria Ramsau mountain church

trips.name: Pinzgau & Zell am See
trips.description: The Pinzgau valley runs east from Zell am See beneath the glaciated flanks of the Hohe Tauern, following an historic salt trade route. Visit the Schmittenhöhe for panoramic views over the Zeller See, drive the Pinzgauer Höhenstrasse ridge road, and explore Kaprun's glacier world above the famous reservoir dams.

stops.name: Maria Alm am Steinernen Meer, Austria
stops.label: Maria Alm

trips.cover_search_query: Zell am See Austria lake mountains Schmittenhöhe

trips.name: Styrian Wine Road
trips.description: Southern Styria's rolling vineyard hills — often called the 'Tuscany of Austria' — produce some of the country's finest whites. The Südsteirische Weinstraße winds past tasting-room cellars, pumpkin-seed oil farms, and the medieval university city of Graz with its UNESCO old town and innovative food scene.
trips.cover_search_query: South Styria wine road Austria vineyards rolling hills

trips.name: Kaiser Mountains & Kitzbühel
trips.description: The Wilder Kaiser's jagged limestone towers are among Austria's most striking massifs — a dramatic backdrop for the affluent medieval ski town of Kitzbühel and the glittering Hintersteiner See. Drive the Kaisergebirgsstrasse, hike the Stripsenjoch, and explore the pretty market towns of Kufstein and St. Johann in Tirol.
trips.cover_search_query: Wilder Kaiser mountains Austria Kitzbühel Tyrol

trips.name: Puster Valley & East Tyrol
trips.description: East Tyrol is Austria's most isolated and unspoiled region — a land of high passes, ancient paths, and the Hohe Tauern's glaciated giants. Drive the Puster Valley to Lienz, Austria's sunniest town, then climb to the Iselsberg Pass for views stretching from the Dolomites to the Glockner group.
trips.cover_search_query: East Tyrol Lienz Austria alpine Dolomitenblick valley

trips.name: Gastein Valley & Thermal Springs
trips.description: The Gastein valley cuts deep into the Hohe Tauern, its warm radon springs rising from ancient granite to supply Europe's finest thermal spas. Bad Gastein's belle-époque villas cling to a gorge above a roaring waterfall, while the Stubnerkogel summit lift delivers some of Austria's most accessible high-alpine terrain.
trips.cover_search_query: Bad Gastein Austria spa belle epoque waterfall valley

trips.name: Waldviertel Granite Plateau
trips.description: The Waldviertel is Austria's most enigmatic landscape — a plateau of granite boulders, dark spruce forests, and carp-filled ponds that rolls toward the Czech border. Explore atmospheric medieval towns like Zwettl and Weitra, visit the magnificent Baroque Stift Altenburg abbey, and discover the scattered stone formations of the Blockheide.
trips.cover_search_query: Waldviertel Austria granite landscape spruce forest medieval

trips.name: Vienna to Burgenland Wine Country
trips.description: From Vienna's Ringstrasse grandeur, drive south through the flat steppe landscape of Burgenland — Austria's warmest and youngest province, bordering Hungary. The reed-fringed Neusiedler See, a UNESCO World Heritage lake, is surrounded by stork-nesting villages and some of Austria's finest red wines.
trips.cover_search_query: Neusiedler See Burgenland Austria lake storks reeds sunset

trips.name: Montafon Valley Drive
trips.description: The Montafon is one of Austria's most handsome alpine valleys — a narrow slot flanked by peaks over 3,000 metres, threaded by the Ill river and dotted with dark timber-clad villages. Drive south from Bludenz to Partenen, the gateway to the Silvretta Alpine Road and its breathtaking reservoir perched among eternal snowfields.
trips.cover_search_query: Silvretta reservoir Austria Montafon alpine road reflections

trips.name: Hohe Tauern Valley Roads
trips.description: The Hohe Tauern National Park is Austria's largest protected area — a cathedral of ice and rock spanning three provinces. This route links the Felbertauern Tunnel, the Iselsberg viewpoint, and the Maltatal's emerald reservoirs, exploring the park's wild heart from valley floors to 2,000m meadow roads.
trips.cover_search_query: Hohe Tauern National Park Austria valley roads glacier mountains

trips.name: Murau & Upper Styria Iron Trail
trips.description: Upper Styria's iron-forging tradition stretches back 2,000 years — the 'Iron Road' follows the Mur river through a landscape of rugged mountains and industrial heritage. Visit Judenburg's medieval tower, Mautern's ironworking museum, and the magnificent baroque castle of Murau, hidden in a broad mountain valley.
trips.cover_search_query: Murau Austria Styria castle valley mountain baroque

trips.name: Mühlviertel & Bohemian Forest
trips.description: The Mühlviertel plateau north of Linz is a gently rolling landscape of granite ridges, mill streams, and linen-weaving villages — a world apart from the alpine drama to the south. Cross into the Bohemian Forest, visit the pilgrimage town of Freistadt with its intact medieval walls, and discover the moody peat bogs of the Schwarzenberger Schwemmkanal.
trips.cover_search_query: Mühlviertel Austria granite landscape spruce forest Bohemian border

trips.name: Enns Valley & Gesäuse Gorge
trips.description: The Gesäuse is Austria's wildest national park — the Enns river tears through a 12km limestone canyon whose walls rise nearly 2,000 metres sheer from the water. Drive the dramatic gorge road, then explore the broad Enns valley's market towns and the towering silhouette of the Hochtor, one of Austria's great alpine faces.
highlights: Admont Abbey world's largest monastery library
trips.cover_search_query: Gesäuse gorge Austria Enns river limestone cliffs national park

trips.name: Lower Inn Valley & Kufstein
trips.description: The Inn river winds between Bavaria and Austria through a valley of medieval river-crossing towns, each with a fortress guarding the old trade routes south. Kufstein's giant mountain fortress rises 90 metres above the river, while Rattenberg is Austria's smallest town — its entire medieval centre preserved in amber.
trips.cover_search_query: Kufstein fortress Austria Inn Valley river Tyrol medieval

trips.name: Carinthian Border Roads & Loibl Pass
trips.description: Southern Carinthia borders Slovenia along a limestone ridge of old frontier roads and alpine meadows. Drive the historic Loibl Pass — the oldest transalpine crossing road — then circle the Karawanken range through Slovenian-speaking villages, past Roman ruins at Virunum, and through the dramatic Ferlach Gorge.
trips.cover_search_query: Carinthia Austria Karawanken mountains pass border Slovenia

trips.name: Bregenzerwald Cheese & Crafts Road
trips.description: The Bregenzerwald is one of Austria's best-kept secrets — a cluster of dark-timbered villages where award-winning architecture and a thriving cheese-making tradition survive side by side. The KäseStrasse links two dozen dairy farms and mountain huts, passing reed-fringed ponds and hand-crafted villages designed by the internationally acclaimed Vorarlberg school of architecture.
trips.cover_search_query: Bregenzerwald Austria alpine village dark timber architecture cheese

trips.name: Salzburg & the Salzburg Surroundings
trips.description: Mozart's birthplace needs no introduction, but the countryside surrounding Salzburg — where 'The Sound of Music' was filmed — is just as spellbinding. Drive the lake-dotted Flachgau, stop at the Hellbrunn trick fountains, climb to Hohensalzburg fortress, and wind through the Tennengau to the Lammertal gorge and the eerie Eisriesenwelt ice cave.
trips.cover_search_query: Salzburg Austria baroque old town Hohensalzburg fortress mountains
